translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Roast in it. Bear it with patience or impatience, it will be the same for you. You will be requited for what you had done."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Enter it – now whether you patiently bear it or are impatient – it is the same for you; for you is a recompense only for what you used to do.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Roast in it! And bear you patiently, or bear not patiently, equal it is to you; you are only being recompensed for that you were working.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Roast therein; endure it or endure it not, thereof it is equal Unto you. Ye are only being requited for that which ye have been working.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Taste you therein its heat, and whether you are patient of it or impatient of it, it is all the same. You are only being requited for what you used to do.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Burn in it. Whether you are patient, or impatient, it is the same for you. You are only being repaid for what you used to do.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Go now and burn in it. It is all the same whether you bear it patiently or do not bear it with patience. You are only being recompensed for your deeds.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Enter therein and whether you are patient of it or impatient of it, it is all the same. You are only being requited for what you used to do.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Endure the heat thereof, and whether ye are patient of it or impatient of it is all one for you. Ye are only being paid for what ye used to do.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Enter it, and it will be the same for you whether you are patient or impatient. You are only being requited for what you used to do.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Roast in it, bear it with or without patience, it is the same, you are only being recompensed for that which you used to do'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Enter to] burn therein; then be patient or impatient - it is all the same for you. You are only being recompensed [for] what you used to do."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Burn in its heat. It is all the same for you whether you exercise patience or not; This is the recompense for your deeds".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Enter into it, then bear (it) patiently, or do not bear (it) patiently, it is the same to you; you shall be requited only (for) what you did.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Now enter it. Whether you behave patiently or impatiently will make no difference: you are only being repaid for what you have done.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Burn ye therein: the same is it to you whether ye bear it with patience, or not: Ye but receive the recompense of your (own) deeds."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Islawha faisbiroo aw la tasbiroo sawaon AAalaykum innama tujzawna ma kuntum taAAmaloona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who fear God and follow the straight path will surely be in gardens and in bliss,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed the pious are in Gardens and peace.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Surely the godfearing shall be in gardens and bliss,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily the God-fearing will bein Gardens and Delight.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2) will be in Gardens (Paradise), and Delight.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But the righteous will be amid gardens and bliss.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Surely the God-fearing shall be in Gardens and bliss,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who have Taqwa will be in Gardens and Delight.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! those who kept their duty dwell in gardens and delight,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and bliss,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: But in the Gardens the righteous shall live in bliss,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, the righteous will be in gardens and pleasure,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The pious will live in bountiful Paradise,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely those who guard (against evil) shall be in gardens and bliss

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Truly, the God-fearing will dwell [on that Day] in gardens and in bliss,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: As to the Righteous, they will be in Gardens, and in Happiness,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna almuttaqeena fee jannatin wanaAAeemin

18.arabic: فَـٰكِهِينَ بِمَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ وَوَقَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْجَحِيمِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Rejoicing at what their Lord has given them; and their Lord will preserve them from the torment of Hell.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Delighted at the bestowal of their Lord; and their Lord has saved them from the fire.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: rejoicing in that their Lord has given them; and their Lord shall guard them against the chastisement of Hell.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Rejoicing in that which their Lord hath vouchsafed Unto them; and their Lord will protect them from the torment of the Flame.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Enjoying in that which their Lord has bestowed on them, and (the fact that) their Lord saved them from the torment of the blazing Fire.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Enjoying what their Lord has given them, and their Lord has spared them the suffering of Hell.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: enjoying what Allah will have endowed them with; and their Lord will have saved them from the torment of the Blazing Fire.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Enjoying in that which their Lord has bestowed on them, and (the fact that) their Lord saved them from the torment of the blazing Fire.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Happy because of what their Lord hath given them, and (because) their Lord hath warded off from them the torment of hell-fire.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: rejoicing because of what their Lord has given them, and that their Lord has saved them from the punishment of hell.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: rejoicing in all their Lord has given them, and their Lord will guard them against the punishment of Hell.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Enjoying what their Lord has given them, and their Lord protected them from the punishment of Hellfire.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: talking of what they have received from their Lord and of how their Lord has saved them from the torment of hell.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Rejoicing because of what their Lord gave them, and their Lord saved them from the punishment of the burning fire.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: rejoicing in whatever their Lord has given them. Their Lord has saved them from the torment of the Fire,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Enjoying the (Bliss) which their Lord hath bestowed on them, and their Lord shall deliver them from the Penalty of the Fire.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fakiheena bima atahum rabbuhum wawaqahum rabbuhum AAathaba aljaheemi

19.arabic: كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ هَنِيٓـًٔۢا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: "Eat and drink with relish," (they will be told), (as recompense) for what you had done."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Eat and drink with pleasure, a reward for what you used to do.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: 'Eat and drink, with wholesome appetite, for that you were working.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Eat and drink with relish for that which ye have been working.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Eat and drink with happiness because of what you used to do."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Eat and drink happily, for what you used to do.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (They will be told): “Eat and drink to your hearts' content as a reward for your deeds.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Eat and drink with happiness because of what you used to do."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (And it is said unto them): Eat and drink in health (as a reward) for what ye used to do,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [They will be told:] ‘Enjoy your food and drink, [as a reward] for what you used to do.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (It will be said): 'Eat and drink with a good appetite because of that which you did'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [They will be told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you used to do."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will be told, "Eat and drink to your heart's delight for what you have done".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Eat and drink pleasantly for what you did,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "Eat and drink with good cheer as a reward for your good deeds,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (To them will be said:) "Eat and drink ye, with profit and health, because of your (good) deeds."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Kuloo waishraboo haneean bima kuntum taAAmaloona

20.arabic: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ سُرُرٍ مَّصْفُوفَةٍ ۖ وَزَوَّجْنَـٰهُم بِحُورٍ عِينٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They would recline on couches set in rows, paired with fair companions (clean of thought and) bright of eye.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Reclining on thrones, in rows; and We have wedded them to maidens with gorgeous eyes.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Reclining upon couches ranged in rows; and We shall espouse them to wide-eyed houris.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Reclining on couches ranged. And We shall couple them with maidens wide eyed.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They will recline (with ease) on thrones arranged in ranks. And We shall marry them to Houris (female, fair ones) with wide lovely eyes.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Relaxing on luxurious furnishings; and We will couple them with gorgeous spouses.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The God-fearing shall be reclining on couches facing each other, and We shall wed them to maidens with large, beautiful eyes.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They will recline on thrones Masfufah. And We shall marry them to Hur (fair females) with wide lovely eyes.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Reclining on ranged couches. And we wed them unto fair ones with wide, lovely eyes.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will be reclining on arrayed couches, and We will wed them to big-eyed houris.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (They shall be) reclining on couches ranged in rows and We shall wed them to houris (virgins of Paradise) with large wide eyes.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will be reclining on thrones lined up, and We will marry them to fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will recline on couches arranged in rows and We shall couple them with maidens with large, lovely eyes.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Reclining on thrones set in lines, and We will unite them to large-eyed beautiful ones.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: reclining on couches arranged in rows. And We shall wed them to fair maidens with large beautiful eyes.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will recline (with ease) on Thrones (of dignity) arranged in ranks; and We shall join them to Companions, with beautiful big and lustrous eyes.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Muttakieena AAala sururin masfoofatin wazawwajnahum bihoorin AAeenin

21.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱتَّبَعَتْهُمْ ذُرِّيَّتُهُم بِإِيمَـٰنٍ أَلْحَقْنَا بِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ وَمَآ أَلَتْنَـٰهُم مِّنْ عَمَلِهِم مِّن شَىْءٍ ۚ كُلُّ ٱمْرِئٍۭ بِمَا كَسَبَ رَهِينٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And those who believed, and whose progeny also followed them in their faith, will be united with their offspring. We will not deprive them of their labour in the least. Every man is bound to what he does.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And those who accepted faith, and whose descendants followed them with faith – We have joined their descendants with them, and have not reduced anything for them from their deeds; every soul is trapped in its own deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And those who believed, and their seed followed them in belief, We shall join their seed with them, and We shall not defraud them of aught of their work; every man shall be pledged for what he earned.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And those who believe and whose progeny follow them in belief. We shall cause their progeny to join them, and We shall not diminish Unto them aught of their own work. Every man is for that which he hath earned a pledge.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And those who believe and whose offspring follow them in Faith, to them shall We join their offspring, and We shall not decrease the reward of their deeds in anything. Every person is a pledge for that which he has earned.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who believed and their offspring followed them in faith—We will unite them with their offspring, and We will not deprive them of any of their works. Every person is hostage to what he has earned.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We shall unite the believers with those descendants of theirs who followed them in their faith, and shall not deny them any part of the reward for their good deeds. Every person is pledged to what he did.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And those who believe and whose offspring follow them in faith, -- to them shall We join their offspring, and We shall not decrease the reward of their deeds in anything. Every person is a pledge for that which he has earned.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they who believe and whose seed follow them in faith, We cause their seed to join them (there), and We deprive them of nought of their (life's) work. Every man is a pledge for that which he hath earned.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The faithful and their descendants who followed them in faith—We will make their descendants join them, and We will not stint anything from [the reward of] their deeds. Every person is hostage to what he has earned.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who believe, and whose descendants follow in belief, We will join their descendants to them. And We will not reduce them of anything of their deeds. Every one is pledged for what he has earned.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And those who believed and whose descendants followed them in faith - We will join with them their descendants, and We will not deprive them of anything of their deeds. Every person, for what he earned, is retained.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The offspring of the believers will also follow them to Paradise. So shall We join their offspring to them because of their faith. We shall reduce nothing from their deeds. Everyone will be responsible for his own actions.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And (as for) those who believe and their offspring follow them in faith, We will unite with them their offspring and We will not diminish to them aught of their work; every man is responsible for what he shall have wrought.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: To those who have attained to faith We shall unite their offspring who have also followed them in faith, and We shall not let any of their good deeds go unrewarded; every human being is a pledge for whatever he has earned.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And those who believe and whose families follow them in Faith,- to them shall We join their families: Nor shall We deprive them (of the fruit) of aught of their works: (Yet) is each individual in pledge for his deeds.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waallatheena amanoo waittabaAAathum thurriyyatuhum bieemanin alhaqna bihim thurriyyatahum wama alatnahum min AAamalihim min shayin kullu imriin bima kasaba raheenun

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We shall give them fruits and meats, and what they desire.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We aided them with fruit and meat, whatever they desire.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We shall succour them with fruits and flesh such as they desire

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We shall increasingly give them fruit and meat such as they desire.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We shall provide them with fruit and meat, such as they desire.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We will supply them with fruit, and meat; such as they desire.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We shall provide them in abundance with all kinds of fruit and meat, whatever they may desire.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We shall provide them with fruit and meat such as they desire.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We provide them with fruit and meat such as they desire.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We will provide them with fruits and meat, such as they desire.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We shall give them fruits and meat as they desire.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We will provide them with fruit and meat from whatever they desire.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We shall provide them with fruits and the meat of the kind which they desire.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We will aid them with fruit and flesh such as they desire.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We shall provide them in abundance with such fruit and meat as they desire.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And We shall bestow on them, of fruit and meat, anything they shall desire.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waamdadnahum bifakihatin walahmin mimma yashtahoona

23.arabic: يَتَنَـٰزَعُونَ فِيهَا كَأْسًا لَّا لَغْوٌ فِيهَا وَلَا تَأْثِيمٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They will exchange cups of wine free of (incitement to) pleasantry or sin.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In it, they accept cups from each other, in which is neither any lewdness nor any sin.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: while they pass therein a cup one to another wherein is no idle talk, no cause of sin,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They will therein snatch from one another a cup; therein will be neither vain babble nor sin.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: There they shall pass from hand to hand a (wine) cup, free from any Laghw (dirty, false, evil vague talk between them), and free from sin (because it will be legal for them to drink).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will exchange therein a cup; wherein is neither harm, nor sin.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They shall pass on to one another a cup that will incite neither levity nor sin.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: There they shall pass from hand to hand a cup, free from any idle talk, and free from sin.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: There they pass from hand to hand a cup wherein is neither vanity nor cause of sin.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: There they will pass from hand to hand a cup wherein there will be neither any vain talk nor sinful speech.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: There they will pass a goblet to one another with neither idle talk nor sin,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will exchange with one another a cup [of wine] wherein [results] no ill speech or commission of sin.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will pass cups of un-intoxicating and unsinful wine to one another.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They shall pass therein from one to another a cup wherein there shall be nothing vain nor any sin.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: There, they shall pass from hand to hand a cup which does not lead to any idle talk or sin.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They shall there exchange, one with another, a (loving) cup free of frivolity, free of all taint of ill.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: YatanazaAAoona feeha kasan la laghwun feeha wala tatheemun

24.arabic: وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ غِلْمَانٌ لَّهُمْ كَأَنَّهُمْ لُؤْلُؤٌ مَّكْنُونٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And young attendants like pearls within their shells, will go round.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And their boy servants shall go around them, as if they were pearls, safely hidden.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and there go round them youths, their own, as if they were hidden pearls.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And there will go round on them youths appointed to attend them as though they were pearls hidden.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And there will go round boy-servants of theirs, to serve them as if they were preserved pearls.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Serving them will be youths like hidden pearls.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Youths as fair as hidden pearls will be set apart to wait upon them; they will be running to and fro to serve them.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And there will go round boy-servants of theirs, to serve them as if they were preserved pearls.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And there go round, waiting on them menservants of their own, as they were hidden pearls.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will be waited upon by their youths, as if they were guarded pearls.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and youths, of their own, shall pass among them as if they were hidden pearls.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: There will circulate among them [servant] boys [especially] for them, as if they were pearls well-protected.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will be served by youths who will be as beautiful as pearls.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And round them shall go boys of theirs as if they were hidden pearls.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will be waited upon by immortal youths, like pearls hidden in their shells.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Round about them will serve, (devoted) to them, young male servants (handsome) as Pearls well-guarded.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wayatoofu AAalayhim ghilmanun lahum kaannahum luluon maknoonun

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They will ask each other questions,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And one of them turned towards the other, questioning.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They advance one upon another, asking each other questions.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they will advance Unto each other asking questions.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And some of them draw near to others, questioning.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And they will approach one another, inquiring.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They will turn to one another and ask (regarding the past events).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And some of them draw near to others, questioning.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And some of them draw near unto others, questioning,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will turn to one another, questioning each other.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They will go to one another asking each other questions:

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they will approach one another, inquiring of each other.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They will turn to one another ask questions,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And some of them shall advance towards others questioning each other.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will converse with one another, putting questions to each other,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will advance to each other, engaging in mutual enquiry.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waaqbala baAAduhum AAala baAAdin yatasaaloona

26.arabic: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا كُنَّا قَبْلُ فِىٓ أَهْلِنَا مُشْفِقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Saying: "We were also once full of fear at home.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Saying, “Indeed before this, we were in our houses, worried.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They say, 'We were before among our people, ever going in fear,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They will say: verily we were aforetime, midst our household, ever in dread.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Saying: "Aforetime, we were afraid with our families (from the punishment of Allah).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will say, “Before this, we were fearful for our families.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: They will say: “When we were living before among our kinsfolk we lived in constant fear (of Allah's displeasure).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Saying: "Aforetime, we were afraid in the midst of our families."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Saying: Lo! of old, when we were with our families, we were ever anxious;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They will say, ‘Indeed, aforetime, we used to be apprehensive about our families.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: 'When we were among our people' they will say, 'we were ever fearful,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will say, "Indeed, we were previously among our people fearful [of displeasing Allah].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: saying, "We were afraid while in the world.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Saying: Surely we feared before on account of our families:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "Before this, when we were among our families, we were full of fear of God's displeasure --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They will say: "Aforetime, we were not without fear for the sake of our people.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qaloo inna kunna qablu fee ahlina mushfiqeena

27.arabic: فَمَنَّ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا وَوَقَىٰنَا عَذَابَ ٱلسَّمُومِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: But God has been gracious to us, and has saved us from the torment of scorching wind.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “So Allah did us a great favour, and saved us from the punishment of the flame.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and God was gracious to us, and guarded us against the chastisement of the burning wind;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore Allah hath obliged us, and hath protected us from the torment of the Scorch.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "But Allah has been gracious to us, and has saved us from the torment of the Fire.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: But God blessed us, and spared us the agony of the Fiery Winds.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then Allah graced us with His favour and saved us from the chastisement of the scorching wind.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "So Allah has been gracious to us, and has saved us from the torment of the Fire."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But Allah hath been gracious unto us and hath preserved us from the torment of the breath of Fire.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: But Allah showed us favour and He saved us from the punishment of the [infernal] miasma.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: but Allah has been gracious to us and has protected us from the punishment of the burning wind.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So Allah conferred favor upon us and protected us from the punishment of the Scorching Fire.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: But God has granted us favors and saved us from the scorching heat of the torment.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: But Allah has been gracious to us and He has saved us from the punishment of the hot wind:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God has been gracious to us and has saved us from the torment of Hell's intense heat --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "But Allah has been good to us, and has delivered us from the Penalty of the Scorching Wind.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Famanna Allahu AAalayna wawaqana AAathaba alssamoomi

28.arabic: إِنَّا كُنَّا مِن قَبْلُ نَدْعُوهُ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْبَرُّ ٱلرَّحِيمُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We used to pray to Him erstwhile; He is the just and merciful.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Indeed we used to worship Him in our previous life; indeed He only is the Benign, the Most Merciful.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: we were before ever calling upon Him; surely He is the All-benign, the All-compassionate.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily we were wont to pray Unto Him aforetime; verily He! it is He, the Benign, the Merciful.'

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Verily, We used to invoke Him (Alone and none else) before. Verily, He is Al-Barr (the Most Subtle, Kind, Courteous, and Generous), the Most Merciful."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Before this, we used to pray to Him. He is the Good, the Compassionate.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Formerly we had always prayed to Him. Surely He is Most Benign, Most Compassionate.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Verily, We used to invoke Him before. Verily, He is the Most Subtle, the Most Merciful."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! we used to pray unto Him of old. Lo! He is the Benign, the Merciful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We used to supplicate Him aforetime. Indeed He is the All-benign, the All-merciful.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Before, we were supplicating to Him. He is the Giving, the Most Merciful'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, we used to supplicate Him before. Indeed, it is He who is the Beneficent, the Merciful."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We had prayed to Him; He is Kind and All-merciful".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely we called upon Him before: Surely He is the Benign, the Merciful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: before this, we used to pray to Him. Surely, He is the Beneficent, the Merciful."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Truly, we did call unto Him from of old: truly it is He, the Beneficent, the Merciful!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna kunna min qablu nadAAoohu innahu huwa albarru alrraheemu

29.arabic: فَذَكِّرْ فَمَآ أَنتَ بِنِعْمَتِ رَبِّكَ بِكَاهِنٍ وَلَا مَجْنُونٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remind them, therefore, that by the grace of your Lord you are neither a soothsayer nor possessed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), enlighten, for by the munificence of your Lord, you are neither a soothsayer nor a madman.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Therefore remind! by thy Lord's blessing thou art not a soothsayer neither possessed.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore admonish thou! thou art not, by the grace of thy Lord, a soothsayer or a madman.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Therefore, remind and preach (mankind, O Muhammad SAW of Islamic Monotheism). By the Grace of Allah, you are neither a soothsayer, nor a madman.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So remind. By the grace of your Lord, you are neither a soothsayer, nor a madman.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So exhort (them, O Prophet), for by your Lord's Grace, you are neither a soothsayer nor a madman.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Therefore, remind. By the grace of Allah, you are neither a Kahin nor a madman.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Therefor warn (men, O Muhammad). By the grace of Allah thou art neither soothsayer nor madman.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So admonish. By your Lord’s blessing, you are not a soothsayer, nor mad.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Therefore, remind. By the Favor of Allah, you are neither a soothsayer, nor mad.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So remind [O Muhammad], for you are not, by the favor of your Lord, a soothsayer or a madman.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), remind them, by the Grace of your Lord, that you are neither a soothsayer or an insane person.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore continue to remind, for by the grace of your Lord, you are not a soothsayer, or a madman.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Therefore continue to give warning, for by the grace of your Lord, you are not a soothsayer or a madman.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Therefore proclaim thou the praises (of thy Lord): for by the Grace of thy Lord, thou art no (vulgar) soothsayer, nor art thou one possessed.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fathakkir fama anta biniAAmati rabbika bikahin wala majnoonin

30.arabic: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ شَاعِرٌ نَّتَرَبَّصُ بِهِۦ رَيْبَ ٱلْمَنُونِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do they say: "He is a poet for whom we expect an adverse turn of fortune?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or they allege, “He is a poet – we await a calamity of the times to befall him.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or do they say, 'He is a poet for whom we await Fate's uncertainty'?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Or say they: a poet for whom we wait some adverse turn of fortune!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or do they say: "(Muhammad SAW is) a poet! We await for him some calamity by time!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or do they say, “A poet—we await for him a calamity of time”?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or do they say: “He is a poet for whom we await an adverse turn of fortune.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or do they say: "A poet! We await for him some calamity by time!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or say they: (he is) a poet, (one) for whom we may expect the accident of time?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they say, ‘[He is] a poet, for whom we await a fatal accident’?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or do they say: 'He is a poet, we are waiting for some misfortune to befall him'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or do they say [of you], "A poet for whom we await a misfortune of time?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they say, "He is only a poet and we are waiting to see him die!?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or do they say: A poet, we wait for him the evil accidents of time.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If they say, "He is but a poet; we are waiting for some misfortune to befall him,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or do they say:- "A Poet! we await for him some calamity (hatched) by Time!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am yaqooloona shaAAirun natarabbasu bihi rayba almanooni

31.arabic: قُلْ تَرَبَّصُوا۟ فَإِنِّى مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُتَرَبِّصِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Tell them: "Keep on expecting, I am expecting with you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Proclaim, “Go on waiting – I too am waiting along with you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'Await! I shall be awaiting with you.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: waits verily I am, with you, among the waiters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW to them): "Wait! I am with you, among the waiters!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “Go on waiting; I will be waiting with you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Tell them: “Wait; I too am waiting with you.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Wait! I am with you among those who wait!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say (unto them): Except (your fill)! Lo! I am with you among the expectant.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Wait! I too am waiting along with you.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Wait if you will; I shall be waiting with you'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Wait, for indeed I am, with you, among the waiters."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say, "Wait, I too am waiting with you".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: Wait, for surely I too with you am of those who wait.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: say [to them], "Wait then: I too am waiting along with you!" --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say thou: "Await ye!- I too will wait along with you!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul tarabbasoo fainnee maAAakum mina almutarabbiseena

32.arabic: أَمْ تَأْمُرُهُمْ أَحْلَـٰمُهُم بِهَـٰذَآ ۚ أَمْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Does their reasoning prompt them to this, or they are a people rebellious?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Is this what their senses tell them, or are they a rebellious people?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or do their intellects bid them do this? Or are they an insolent people?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Enjoin them their understandings to this? or are they a people conumacious?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Do their minds command them this [i.e. to tell a lie against you (Muhammad SAW)] or are they people exceeding the bounds (i.e. from Belief in Allah to disbelief).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or is it that their dreams compel them to this? Or are they aggressive people?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do their minds prompt them to say such things, or are they a people immersed in transgression?”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or do their minds command them this or are they people transgressing all bounds

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Do their minds command them to do this, or are they an outrageous folk?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Is it their intellect which prompts them to [say] this, or are they a rebellious lot?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, do their intellects order them to do this? Or, are they an insolent people?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or do their minds command them to [say] this, or are they a transgressing people?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Does their reason tell them to say this or is it because they are a rebellious people?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Nay! do their understandings bid them this? Or are they an inordinate people?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: is it their minds that prompt them [to say] this, or are they merely insolent people?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Is it that their faculties of understanding urge them to this, or are they but a people transgressing beyond bounds?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am tamuruhum ahlamuhum bihatha am hum qawmun taghoona

33.arabic: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ تَقَوَّلَهُۥ ۚ بَل لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or do they say: "He has fabricated it?" In fact, they will never believe.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: What! They say, “He has invented the Qur'an”? Rather they do not have faith.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or do they say, 'He has invented it?' Nay, but they do not believe.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Or say they: he hath fabricated it: aye they will not believe.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or do they say: "He (Muhammad SAW) has forged it (this Quran)?" Nay! They believe not!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or do they say, “He made it up”? Rather, they do not believe.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do they say: “He has himself fabricated the Qur'an?” No; the truth is that they are altogether averse to believing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or do they say: "He has forged it" Nay! They believe not!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or say they: He hath invented it? Nay, but they will not believe!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they say, ‘He has improvised it [himself]?’ Rather, they have no faith!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do they say: 'He has invented it' No, they do not believe.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or do they say, "He has made it up"? Rather, they do not believe.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they say, "He has falsely invented it (the Quran)?" In fact, they themselves have no faith.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or do they say: He has forged it. Nay! they do not believe.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Or do they say, "He has invented it himself"? Indeed, they are not willing to believe.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or do they say, "He fabricated the (Message)"? Nay, they have no faith!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am yaqooloona taqawwalahu bal la yuminoona

34.arabic: فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِحَدِيثٍ مِّثْلِهِۦٓ إِن كَانُوا۟ صَـٰدِقِينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So let them bring a discourse like it, if they are truthful

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So let them bring a single discourse like it, if they are truthful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then let them bring a discourse like it, if they speak truly.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Let them bring a discourse like thereUnto, if they say sooth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Let them then produce a recital like unto it (the Quran) if they are truthful.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So let them produce a discourse like it, if they are truthful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (If they are truthful in this), then let them produce a discourse of similar splendour.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Let them then produce a recitation like unto it if they are truthful.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then let them produce speech the like thereof, if they are truthful.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Let them bring a discourse like it, if they are truthful.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Let them produce a phrase like it, if what they say is true!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then let them produce a statement like it, if they should be truthful.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Let them produce a discourse like it if they are true in their claim.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then let them bring an announcement like it if they are truthful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Let them produce a scripture like it, if what they say is true.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Let them then produce a recital like unto it,- If (it be) they speak the truth!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falyatoo bihadeethin mithlihi in kanoo sadiqeena

35.arabic: أَمْ خُلِقُوا۟ مِنْ غَيْرِ شَىْءٍ أَمْ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰلِقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or were they created of themselves? Or are they the creators?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Have they not been created from some source, or are they themselves the creators?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or were they created out of nothing? Or are they the creators?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Have they not been created by aught, or are they the creators?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Were they created by nothing, or were they themselves the creators?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or were they created out of nothing? Or are they the creators?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Did they come into being without any creator? Or were they their own creators?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or were they created by nothing Or were they themselves the creators

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or were they created out of naught? Or are they the creators?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Were they created from nothing? Or are they [their own] creators?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, were they created out of nothing? Or, were they their own creators?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or were they created by nothing, or were they the creators [of themselves]?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Have they been created from nothing or are they themselves their own creators?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or were they created without there being anything, or are they the creators?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Were they created out of nothing, or are they their own creators?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Were they created of nothing, or were they themselves the creators?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am khuliqoo min ghayri shayin am humu alkhaliqoona

36.arabic: أَمْ خَلَقُوا۟ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ ۚ بَل لَّا يُوقِنُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or have they created the heavens and the earth? In fact they are certain of nothing.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather they are not certain.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and earth? Nay, but they have not sure faith.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Created they the heavens and the earth! Aye! they will not be convinced.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Nay, but they have no firm Belief.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? In fact, they are not certain.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or is it they who created the heavens and the earth? No; the truth is that they lack sure faith.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth Nay, but they have no firm belief.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Nay, but they are sure of nothing!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they have no certainty!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, did they create the heavens and the earth? No, their belief is not certain!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they are not certain.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Have they created the heavens and the earth? In fact, they have no strong faith.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Nay! they have no certainty.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Did they create the heavens and the earth? No! They have no faith.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or did they create the heavens and the earth? Nay, they have no firm belief.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am khalaqoo alssamawati waalarda bal la yooqinoona

37.arabic: أَمْ عِندَهُمْ خَزَآئِنُ رَبِّكَ أَمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُصَۣيْطِرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do they possess the treasures of your Lord? Or are they the treasurers?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or do they have the treasures of your Lord, or are they the authority?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or are thy Lord's treasuries in their keeping? Or are they the registrars?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Are with them the treasures of thy Lord! or are they the dispensers?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or are with them the treasures of your Lord? Or are they the tyrants with the authority to do as they like?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or do they possess the treasuries of your Lord? Or are they the controllers?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or do they have your Lord's treasures in their keeping? Or have absolute authority over them?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or are with them the treasures of your Lord Or are they the tyrants with the authority to do as they like

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or do they own the treasures of thy Lord? Or have they been given charge (thereof)?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they possess the treasuries of your Lord? Or do they control [them]?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, are the treasures of your Lord in their keeping? Or, are they the controllers?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or have they the depositories [containing the provision] of your Lord? Or are they the controllers [of them]?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they own the treasures of your Lord? Have they any authority over God?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or have they the treasures of your Lord with them? Or have they been set in absolute authority?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do they own the treasures of your Lord, or have they been given charge of them?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or are the Treasures of thy Lord with them, or are they the managers (of affairs)?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am AAindahum khazainu rabbika am humu almusaytiroona

38.arabic: أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ ۖ فَلْيَأْتِ مُسْتَمِعُهُم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or do they have a ladder (climbing which) they can hear (the secrets of heaven)? Let one who has heard then bring a clear proof.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or do they have any stairs, climbing upon which they eavesdrop? So their eavesdropper should bring some clear proof!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or have they a ladder whereon they listen? Then let any of them that has listened bring a clear authority.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Have they a stairway whereby they overhear? Then let their listener bring an authority manifest.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or have they a stairway (to heaven), by means of which they listen (to the talks of the angels)? Then let their listener produce some manifest proof.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or do they have a stairway by means of which they listen? Then let their listener produce a clear proof.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or do they have a ladder whereon they can climb and attempt to listen (to what is transpiring in the Higher Realm)? Then, let any of them who has listened to it produce a clear proof of it.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or have they a stairway (to heaven), by means of which they listen Then let their listener produce some manifest proof.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or have they any stairway (unto heaven) by means of which they overhear (decrees). Then let their listener produce some warrant manifest!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they have a ladder [leading up to heaven] whereby they eavesdrop? If so, let their eavesdropper produce a manifest authority.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, do they have a ladder on which they listen? Then let any of them that has listened bring a clear authority.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or have they a stairway [into the heaven] upon which they listen? Then let their listener produce a clear authority.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they have a ladder (by which they can climb up to the heavens) and listen (to the angels) and come back to the rest of the people with clear authority?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or have they the means by which they listen? Then let their listener bring a clear authority.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Have they a ladder up to heaven by means of which they can overhear? Then let their listeners bring a clear proof.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or have they a ladder, by which they can (climb up to heaven and) listen (to its secrets)? Then let (such a) listener of theirs produce a manifest proof.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am lahum sullamun yastamiAAoona feehi falyati mustamiAAuhum bisultanin mubeenin

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Has (God) daughters and they sons?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: What! The daughters for Him, and the sons for you?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or has He daughters, and they sons?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Hath He daughters and ye sons?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or has He (Allah) only daughters and you have sons?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or for Him the daughters, and for you the sons?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or does Allah have daughters whereas you have sons?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or has He only daughters and you have sons

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or hath He daughters whereas ye have sons?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Does He have daughters while you have sons?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, has He daughters, and they sons?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or has He daughters while you have sons?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do the daughters belong to Him and the sons to you?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or has He daughters while you have sons?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Does God have daughters while you have sons?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or has He only daughters and ye have sons?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am lahu albanatu walakumu albanoona

40.arabic: أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or do you demand any wages from them so that they are burdened with expense?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or do you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) ask any fee from them, so they are burdened with the penalty?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or askest thou them for a wage, and so they are weighed down with debt?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Or askest thou a hire from them so that they are with debt laden?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or is it that you (O Muhammad SAW) ask a wage from them (for your preaching of Islamic Monotheism) so that they are burdened with a load of debt?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or do you demand a payment from them, and they are burdened by debt?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or is it that you ask of them any recompense so that they should fear to be weighed down under the burden of debt?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or is it that you ask a wage from them so that they are burdened with a load of debt

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or askest thou (Muhammad) a fee from them so that they are plunged in debt?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do you ask them for a reward, so that they are [wary of] being weighed down with debt?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, do you ask them for a wage, so that they become weighed down in debt?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or do you, [O Muhammad], ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do you (Muhammad) ask them for any payment (for your preaching) which they cannot afford?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or do you ask them for a reward, so that they are overburdened by a debt?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Or do you ask them for a reward, so that they are over-burdened by debt?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or is it that thou dost ask for a reward, so that they are burdened with a load of debt?-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am tasaluhum ajran fahum min maghramin muthqaloona

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or do they have knowledge of the Unknown which they write down?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or is the hidden with them, by which they pass judgements?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or is the Unseen in their keeping, and so they are writing it down?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Is with them the Unseen, and they write it down!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or that the Ghaib (unseen) is with them, and they write it down?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or do they know the future, and they are writing it down?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or is it that they have access to (the Truths in) the realm beyond sense-perception which they are writing down?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or that the Unseen is with them, and they write it down

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or possess they the Unseen so that they can write (it) down?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they have [access to] the Unseen, which they write down?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, is the Unseen in their keeping, so they are writing it down?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they have knowledge of the unseen, thus, are able to predict (the future)?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or have they the unseen so that they write (it) down?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do they possess knowledge of the unseen, so that they can write it down?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or that the Unseen in it their hands, and they write it down?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am AAindahumu alghaybu fahum yaktuboona

42.arabic: أَمْ يُرِيدُونَ كَيْدًا ۖ فَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْمَكِيدُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or do they want to stage a deception? -- Then only those who do not believe will be the ones who will be tricked.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or are they planning a conspiracy? So the conspiracy will befall only upon the disbelievers.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or desire they to outwit? The unbelievers, they are the outwitted.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Seek they to lay a plot? Then those who disbelieve it is they who shall be plotted against.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or do they intend a plot (against you O Muhammad SAW)? But those who disbelieve (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism) are themselves in a plot!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or are they planning a conspiracy? The conspiracy will befall the disbelievers.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Or are they contriving a stratagem against you? If so, that stratagem will rebound against the unbelievers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or do they intend a plot But those who disbelieve are themselves plotted against!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or seek they to ensnare (the messenger)? But those who disbelieve, they are the ensnared!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they seek to outmaneuver [Allah]? But it is the faithless who are the outmaneuvered ones!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, do they desire to outwit? The unbelievers are the outwitted.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or do they intend a plan? But those who disbelieve - they are the object of a plan.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they design evil plans? The disbelievers themselves will be snared by their evil plots.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or do they desire a war? But those who disbelieve shall be the vanquished ones in war.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do they want to hatch some plot? Those who deny the truth will be the victims of the plot.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or do they intend a plot (against thee)? But those who defy Allah are themselves involved in a Plot!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am yureedoona kaydan faallatheena kafaroo humu almakeedoona

43.arabic: أَمْ لَهُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Or do they have a god apart from God? He is too exalted for what they associate with Him!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Or do they have a God besides Allah? Purity is to Allah from their ascribing of partners to Him.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or have they a god, other than God? Glory be to God, above that which they associate!

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Is theirs a god beside Allah? Hallowed be Allah from that which they associate!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or have they an ilah (a god) other than Allah? Glorified be Allah from all that they ascribe as partners (to Him)

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or do they have a god besides God? God transcends what they associate.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do they have any god other than Allah? Exalted be Allah above whatever they associate (with Him in His Divinity).

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or have they a god other than Allah Glorified be Allah from all that they ascribe as partners.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or have they any god beside Allah? Glorified be Allah from all that they ascribe as partner (unto Him)!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do they have any god other than Allah? Clear is Allah of any partners that they may ascribe [to Him]!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, do they have a god, other than Allah? Exaltations to Allah above that which they associate!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or have they a deity other than Allah? Exalted is Allah above whatever they associate with Him.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do they have another god besides God? God is too exalted to be considered equal to the idols.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or have they a god other than Allah? Glory be to Allah from what they set up (with Him).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Or have they a god other than God? Exalted be God over what they ascribe as partners [to Him]!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or have they a god other than Allah? Exalted is Allah far above the things they associate with Him!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am lahum ilahun ghayru Allahi subhana Allahi AAamma yushrikoona

44.arabic: وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ كِسْفًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ سَاقِطًا يَقُولُوا۟ سَحَابٌ مَّرْكُومٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If they should see a segment falling from the sky, they would say: "It is only a massive cloud."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And were they to see a portion of the sky falling, they will say, “It is a heap of clouds.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Even if they saw lumps falling from heaven, they would say, 'A massed cloud!'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And if they should see a fragment of the heaven falling down, they would say: it is only clouds piled up.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And if they were to see a piece of the heaven falling down, they would say: "Clouds gathered in heaps!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Even if they were to see lumps of the sky falling down, they would say, “A mass of clouds.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (So obstinate are they that) even if they were to see some fragments of the sky falling down they would still say: “It is only a mass of cloud.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And if they were to see a piece of the heaven falling down, they would say: "Clouds gathered in heaps!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And if they were to see a fragment of the heaven falling, they would say: A heap of clouds.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Were they to see a fragment falling from the sky, they would say, ‘A cumulous cloud.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Even if they saw lumps falling from the sky they would say: 'A massed cloud'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And if they were to see a fragment from the sky falling, they would say, "[It is merely] clouds heaped up."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Even if they were to see a part of the heavens falling down upon them, they would say, "It is only dense cloud".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And if they should see a portion of the heaven coming down, they would say: Piled up clouds.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If they should see a part of the heavens falling down, they would say, "A mass of clouds,"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Were they to see a piece of the sky falling (on them), they would (only) say: "Clouds gathered in heaps!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wain yaraw kisfan mina alssamai saqitan yaqooloo sahabun markoomun

45.arabic: فَذَرْهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يُلَـٰقُوا۟ يَوْمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى فِيهِ يُصْعَقُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So, leave them until they face their day (of doom) when they will be stunned.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore leave them, until they confront their day, in which they will be stunned.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then leave them, till they encounter their day wherein they shall be thunderstruck,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore let them alone, till they meet their Day whereon they shall swoon.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So leave them alone till they meet their Day, in which they will sink into a fainting (with horror).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So leave them until they meet their Day in which they will be stunned.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So leave them alone until they encounter that Day of theirs when they shall be struck down,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So leave them alone till they meet their Day, in which they will sink into a fainting (with horror).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then let them be (O Muhammad), till they meet their day, in which they will be thunder-stricken,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So leave them until they encounter the day when they will be thunderstruck,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: So leave them till they encounter their Day in which they shall be thunderstruck.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So leave them until they meet their Day in which they will be struck insensible -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: So leave them alone until they face the day when they will be struck dead from terror

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Leave them then till they meet that day of theirs wherein they shall be made to swoon (with terror):

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: so leave them alone till they face the Day on which they will be struck dumb,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So leave them alone until they encounter that Day of theirs, wherein they shall (perforce) swoon (with terror),-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fatharhum hatta yulaqoo yawmahumu allathee feehi yusAAaqoona

46.arabic: يَوْمَ لَا يُغْنِى عَنْهُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Their deception will not avail them in the least on that day, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: A day on which their scheming will not benefit them at all, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: the day when their guile shall avail them naught, and they shall not be helped.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: A Day whereon their plotting will avail them not at all, nor will they be succoured.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The Day when their plotting shall not avail them at all nor will they be helped (i.e. they will receive their torment in Hell).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The Day when their ploys will avail them nothing; and they will not be helped.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: when their stratagem shall be of no avail to them, nor shall they be succoured.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The Day when their plotting shall not avail them at all, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: A day in which their guile will naught avail them, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: the day when their guile will not avail them in any way, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The Day when their guile shall not relieve them a thing, and they shall not be helped.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The Day their plan will not avail them at all, nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and when their evil plans will be of no benefit to them nor will they be helped.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The day on which their struggle shall not avail them aught, nor shall they be helped.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: the Day when none of their scheming will be of the least avail to them, and they will receive no succour.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The Day when their plotting will avail them nothing and no help shall be given them.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yawma la yughnee AAanhum kayduhum shayan wala hum yunsaroona

47.arabic: وَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ عَذَابًا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Surely there are other torments besides this for those who are wicked, though most of them do not know.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed for the unjust is another punishment before this, but most of them do not know. (Punishment in the grave is proven by this verse.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And there surely awaits the evildoers a chastisement beyond even that, but most of them know it not.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And verily for those who do wrong there is a torment before that; but most of them know not.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And verily, for those who do wrong, there is another punishment (i.e. the torment in this world and in their graves) before this, but most of them know not. [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 27, Page 36].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: For those who do wrong, there is a punishment besides that; but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Surely a chastisement awaits the wrong-doers even before the coming of that Day; but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And verily, for those who do wrong, there is another punishment before this; but most of them know not.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And verily, for those who do wrong, there is a punishment beyond that. But most of them know not.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed there is a punishment besides that for those who do wrong, but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: For the harmdoers there is indeed, a punishment before that, but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And indeed, for those who have wronged is a punishment before that, but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The unjust will suffer other torments besides this but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And surely those who are unjust shall have a punishment besides that (in the world), but most of them do not know.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Truly, for those who do wrong there is a punishment besides that, though most of them do not know it.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And verily, for those who do wrong, there is another punishment besides this: But most of them understand not.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wainna lillatheena thalamoo AAathaban doona thalika walakinna aktharahum la yaAAlamoona

48.arabic: وَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ فَإِنَّكَ بِأَعْيُنِنَا ۖ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Await the judgement of your Lord, for you are always before Our eyes, and glorify your Lord with praises when you rise,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And be patient upon your Lord’s command, (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), for you are indeed in Our sight; and proclaim the Purity of your Lord while praising Him, whenever you stand.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And be thou patient under the judgment of thy Lord; surely thou art before Our eyes. And proclaim the praise of thy Lord when thou arisest,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And wait thou patiently the judgment of thy Lord; verily thou art before Our eyes; and hallow the praise of thy Lord when thou uprisest.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So wait patiently (O Muhammad SAW) for the Decision of your Lord, for verily, you are under Our Eyes, and glorify the Praises of your Lord when you get up from sleep.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So patiently await the decision of your Lord, for you are before Our Eyes; and proclaim the praises of your Lord when you arise.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Be patient, then, (O Prophet), until the judgement of your Lord comes. For surely you are before Our eyes. And celebrate the praise of your Lord when you rise,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So wait patiently for the decision of your Lord, for verily, you are under Our Eyes; and glorify the praises of your Lord when you get up.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So wait patiently (O Muhammad) for thy Lord's decree, for surely thou art in Our sight; and hymn the praise of thy Lord when thou uprisest,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So submit patiently to the judgement of your Lord, for indeed you fare before Our eyes. And celebrate the praise of your Lord when you rise [at dawn],

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And be patient under the Judgement of your Lord, surely, you are before Our Eyes. And exalt with the praise of your Lord when you arise,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And be patient, [O Muhammad], for the decision of your Lord, for indeed, you are in Our eyes. And exalt [Allah] with praise of your Lord when you arise.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Wait patiently for the command of your Lord. We are watching over you. Glorify your Lord when you rise during the night

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And wait patiently for the judgment of your Lord, for surely you are before Our eyes, and sing the praise of your Lord when you rise;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So wait patiently for the Judgement of your Lord -- you are certainly under Our watchful eye. And glorify and celebrate the praises of your Lord when you rise up [from your sleep].

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Now await in patience the command of thy Lord: for verily thou art in Our eyes: and celebrate the praises of thy Lord the while thou standest forth,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waisbir lihukmi rabbika fainnaka biaAAyunina wasabbih bihamdi rabbika heena taqoomu

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And glorify Him in the night and when the stars begin to wane.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And proclaim His Purity during the night, and when the stars turn back.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and proclaim the praise of thy Lord in the night, and at the declining of the stars.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And in the night also hallow Him, and at the setting of the stars.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And in the night time, also glorify His Praises, and at the setting of the stars.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And glorify Him during the night, and at the receding of the stars.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and also celebrate His praise at night, and at the retreat of the stars.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And in the nighttime also glorify His praises and at the setting of the stars.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And in the night-time also hymn His praise, and at the setting of the stars.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and also glorify Him during the night and at the receding of the stars.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and exalt Him in the night and at the declining of the stars.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And in a part of the night exalt Him and after [the setting of] the stars.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and glorify Him after the setting of the stars.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And in the night, give Him glory too, and at the setting of the stars.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Extol His glory at night, and at the setting of the stars.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And for part of the night also praise thou Him,- and at the retreat of the stars!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamina allayli fasabbihhu waidbara alnnujoomi
